content: Just as the English language concept of reflexion frames human learning as a constant process of turning past outputs back into reflective input to shape better future thinking, the LLM self-refinement framework can be reimagined as building a grammatical 'core vocabulary' of high-quality past reflection outcomes stored in dynamic memory, rather than just refining single isolated outputs. This turns iterative self-feedback into a cumulative language-like learning process that grows richer over time.
